steps.title: Spot the turning point
steps.content: The passage begins by describing Bill asking daily about seafaring men, then pivots: “but at last we began to see he was desirous to avoid them.” This explicit statement reverses the initial assumption and answers the question directly.

steps.title: Match the answer to his behavior
steps.content: To explain why Bill asks daily, we need a reason that fits his actions: checking through the curtain and staying silent around sailors. Choice D supplies this—he doesn't want unexpected encounters. His cautious conduct contradicts A and B (seeking company or employment) and reveals his concern is personal, not protective of guests (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
